commands.review_summary:
The PR adds TUI handling for Gateway session.message events, queues/coalesces active-session history reloads, and covers the new behavior in TUI handler tests.

PR surface: Source +122, Tests +266. Total +388 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main subscribes the TUI to session events but ignores the Gateway session.message event that carries displayable transcript updates. I did not run a live terminal reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof plus normal maintainer review; there is no narrow automation repair to queue from this pass.

target.title: fix(tui): handle live session message updates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/38829. The Gateway already emits live `session.message` events when a session transcript gets a displayable message, but the TUI only handled `chat`, `agent`, `btw`, and `sessions.changed`, so current-session messages from another surface could remain invisible until a reload or session switch.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Adds a typed TUI handler for existing `session.message` events and wires it into the websocket dispatcher.
- Reloads the current session history when the selected session receives a live transcript message while the TUI is locally idle.
- Coalesces overlapping history reloads and drops queued follow-up reloads if the selected session/agent changes before the queued reload runs.
- Defers live message reloads during local active/pending runs, flushing only silent-final cases and clearing unproven displayable-final refreshes so a just-rendered assistant final is not wiped by an uncorrelated history rebuild.

### Design note: subscription reuse

This reuses the existing `sessions.subscribe` session-event subscription (the TUI already joins `sessionEventSubscribers` for `sessions.changed`, and the Gateway broadcasts `session.message` to that same set), rather than adding a new targeted `sessions.messages.subscribe` controller. That keeps the change to a client-side consumer with no new subscribe/unsubscribe protocol or server fanout change. Trade-off: the TUI receives `session.message` for any session in its broad subscription and filters client-side, vs a per-session-key subscription. This is functionally fine for a single-session TUI (negligible extra traffic) and is a possible future efficiency optimization, not a correctness gap.

## User Impact

Users viewing a TUI session now see live transcript messages appended from other surfaces without leaving and reopening the session. The change also preserves the existing displayable-final guard so local active responses do not disappear because of an unsafe history refresh.

## Evidence

- `/Users/harjothk/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` - 90 tests passed
- `/Users/harjothk/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` - 2 shards passed before the final TUI-only deferral simplification
- `PATH=/Users/harjothk/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui-types.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `PATH=/Users/harjothk/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH /Users/harjothk/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui-types.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` - passed before the final TUI-only deferral simplification
- Two-lens review rechecked the earlier blockers: adversarial implementer and architect steward both cleared their prior `runId`, local-idle reload, and queued reload scoping findings. Repo local autoreview was attempted repeatedly; the final stalled pass was skipped by explicit user instruction, and normal push hooks were not bypassed.

AI assistance disclosure: implemented with Codex and reviewed with Codex-assisted local/subagent review.
